metadata.name: TOKEN_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This table records all token transfers on the FLOW blockchain from after the Crescendo upgrade.

DEPOSITED_UUID_ROOT.name: DEPOSITED_UUID_ROOT
DEPOSITED_UUID_ROOT.comment: The UUID of the transfer that was deposited to the recipient. This is the root deposit, as certain transfers that utilize a Vault may have two subsequent Deposited events.

WITHDRAWN_UUID_ROOT.name: WITHDRAWN_UUID_ROOT
WITHDRAWN_UUID_ROOT.comment: The UUID of the transfer that was withdrawn from the sender's account. This is the root withdrawal, as certain transfers that utilize a Vault may have two subsequent Withdrawal events.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

TOKEN_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN_CONTRACT
TOKEN_CONTRACT.comment: The contract address for a token on the Flow blockchain.

AMOUNT_ADJ.name: AMOUNT_ADJ
AMOUNT_ADJ.comment: An amount with decimal adjustment applied.

FROM_ADDRESS_BALANCE_AFTER.name: FROM_ADDRESS_BALANCE_AFTER
FROM_ADDRESS_BALANCE_AFTER.comment: The token balance of the withdrawn token in the sender account after the transfer.

TO_ADDRESS_BALANCE_AFTER.name: TO_ADDRESS_BALANCE_AFTER
TO_ADDRESS_BALANCE_AFTER.comment: The token balance of the deposited token in the recipient account after the transfer.

IS_FEE_TRANSFER.name: IS_FEE_TRANSFER
IS_FEE_TRANSFER.comment: A boolean field indicating if the transfer was the transaction fee payment, as assessed by to_uuid = 0

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Transaction status, if it succeeded or failed.

TOKEN_TRANSFERS_ID.name: TOKEN_TRANSFERS_ID
TOKEN_TRANSFERS_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

row_count.label: Row Count
row_count.description: An approximate count of rows in this table

clustering_key.description: The key used to cluster this table

metadata.name: TRANSACTION_ACTORS
metadata.comment: -| A curated table that extracts addresses involved in the events of a transactions, tagging them as actors in the transaction.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block number, corresponds with height.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

PAYER.name: PAYER
PAYER.comment: Address of the wallet paying for the transaction.

EVENT_TYPE.name: EVENT_TYPE
EVENT_TYPE.comment: The type of method called on the event_contract. In the fact_events table, this represents the method called on a contract. This may also reference the full user-defined event type from the event object. See more on this in the Flow docs: https://developers.flow.com/build/basics/events#user-defined-events.

EVENT_INDEX.name: EVENT_INDEX
EVENT_INDEX.comment: The index of the event within the transaction, i.e. in what order the events occurred.

ARGUMENT_NAME.name: ARGUMENT_NAME
ARGUMENT_NAME.comment: The name of the key for the argument in the event payload emitted by the transaction event.

ADDRESS.name: ADDRESS
ADDRESS.comment: The on-chain address. See more in the Flow docs on accounts and addresses: https://developers.flow.com/build/basics/accounts and https://developers.flow.com/evm/accounts

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Transaction status, if it succeeded or failed.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_ID.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_ID
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_ID.comment:
Partition column used for efficient querying.  
`ROUND(block_number, -5)`

TRANSACTION_ACTORS_ID.name: TRANSACTION_ACTORS_ID
TRANSACTION_ACTORS_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: BLOCKS_COMPLETENESS
metadata.comment: Observability model that queries the blocks table at a designated interval to record and track the completeness of the data.

TEST_NAME.name: TEST_NAME
TEST_NAME.comment: Name of the model being tested.

MIN_BLOCK.name: MIN_BLOCK
MIN_BLOCK.comment: The min block in the test.

MAX_BLOCK.name: MAX_BLOCK
MAX_BLOCK.comment: The max block height in the test.

MIN_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: MIN_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
MIN_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The minimum block timestamp for the test.

MAX_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: MAX_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
MAX_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The max block timestamp in the test.

BLOCKS_IMPACTED_COUNT.name: BLOCKS_IMPACTED_COUNT
BLOCKS_IMPACTED_COUNT.comment: Number of blocks impacted by a gap in the test.

BLOCKS_IMPACTED_ARRAY.name: BLOCKS_IMPACTED_ARRAY
BLOCKS_IMPACTED_ARRAY.comment: Array of block numbers that were impacted during the test.

metadata.name: DIM_ASSET_METADATA
metadata.comment: A comprehensive dimensional table holding asset metadata and other relevant details pertaining to each id, from multiple providers. This data set includes raw, non-transformed data coming directly from the provider APIs and rows are not intended to be unique. As a result, there may be data quality issues persisting in the APIs that flow through to this dimensional model. If you are interested in using a curated data set instead, please utilize ez_asset_metadata.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The specific address representing the asset on a specific platform. This will be NULL if referring to a native asset.

ASSET_ID.name: ASSET_ID
ASSET_ID.comment: The unique identifier representing the asset.

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: The Blockchain, Network, or Platform for this asset.

BLOCKCHAIN_ID.name: BLOCKCHAIN_ID
BLOCKCHAIN_ID.comment: The unique identifier of the Blockchain, Network, or Platform for this asset.

PROVIDER.name: PROVIDER
PROVIDER.comment: The provider or source of the data.

DIM_ASSET_METADATA_ID.name: DIM_ASSET_METADATA_ID
DIM_ASSET_METADATA_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

metadata.name: FACT_DEX_SWAPS
metadata.comment: This table records asset swaps on the Flow blockchain.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment:
The smart contract address of the DEX swap pool.
Note for metapier swaps in the labels table - they all appear to use the same master contract `A.609e10301860b683.PierPair` so the `pool_id` is essential to differentiating what pool is used in the swap route.

SWAP_INDEX.name: SWAP_INDEX
SWAP_INDEX.comment: The positioning of the swap within a transaction. The first swap will index at 0. A swap transaction may only run through 1 pool, in which case the max index will be 0. A multi-pool swap that runs through 2 pools, for example, will have 2 records with swap index 0 and 1.

TRADER.name: TRADER
TRADER.comment: The account address of the trader performing the swap action.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: Protocol on which the event (dex swap, nft trade) occurred, where able to determine.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The source address of the token out in the swap record. If this is the first token withdraw in a swap, then the address is likely to be the trader. This could also be the address of a pool if this swap index is later in the transaction.

TOKEN_OUT.name: TOKEN_OUT
TOKEN_OUT.comment:
The smart contract address for the token that is being swapped out of the traders wallet.
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

AMOUNT_OUT.name: AMOUNT_OUT
AMOUNT_OUT.comment:
The amount of the token being swapped that the trader is sending to the pool (selling).
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The destination address of the token in for the swap index. If this is the initial withdraw/deposit event interaction, it is likely to be the pool address for the swap. If this is the final destination for the token, this is likely to be the trader's address.

TOKEN_IN.name: TOKEN_IN
TOKEN_IN.comment:
The smart contract address for the token that is being swapped into the traders wallet.
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

AMOUNT_IN.name: AMOUNT_IN
AMOUNT_IN.comment:
The amount of the token being swapped that the trader is receiving from the pool (buying).
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

FACT_DEX_SWAPS_ID.name: FACT_DEX_SWAPS_ID
FACT_DEX_SWAPS_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Transaction status, if it succeeded or failed.

ERROR_MSG.name: ERROR_MSG

metadata.name: NFT_SALES_S
metadata.comment: NFT market sales on the Flow blockchain. This table will only contain successful transactions, as failed transactions will not have the requisite events to determine it was an attempted NFT purchase.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

MARKETPLACE.name: MARKETPLACE
MARKETPLACE.comment: Contract address for the marketplace where the transaction occurred. Flow uses a general-purpose contract at `A.4eb8a10cb9f87357.NFTStorefront` for a significant number of sales. This column does not necessarily indicate the website or specific platform on which the sale occurred.

NFT_COLLECTION.name: NFT_COLLECTION
NFT_COLLECTION.comment: The contract address or ID for the NFT Collection.

NFT_ID.name: NFT_ID
NFT_ID.comment: The id of the NFT, usually a number.

BUYER.name: BUYER
BUYER.comment: The party in the transaction purchasing the asset, this is an account address.

SELLER.name: SELLER
SELLER.comment: The party in the transaction selling the asset, this is an account address.

PRICE.name: PRICE
PRICE.comment: The price of the asset, in the currency specified in the table.

CURRENCY.name: CURRENCY
CURRENCY.comment: The contract address refering to the currency used in the transaction. This may or may not relate to a token in the prices table, depending on the marketplace or transaction.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Transaction status, if it succeeded or failed.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Time the record was inserted into snowflake

TOKENFLOW.name: TOKENFLOW
TOKENFLOW.comment: An array of events tracking the flow of tokens, in event_index order, for a transaction. This is constructed from the following events within a transaction: `TokensDeposited`, `TokensWithdrawn`, `ForwardedDeposit`.

COUNTERPARTIES.name: COUNTERPARTIES
COUNTERPARTIES.comment: An array containing the addresses interacted with during the tokenflow of the transaction. This contains all payers and recipients of tokens within the transaction.

NFT_SALES_ID.name: NFT_SALES_ID
NFT_SALES_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: EZ_DEX_SWAPS
metadata.comment: This table records asset swaps on the Flow blockchain, with price and label information.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment:
The smart contract address of the DEX swap pool.
Note for metapier swaps in the labels table - they all appear to use the same master contract `A.609e10301860b683.PierPair` so the `pool_id` is essential to differentiating what pool is used in the swap route.

SWAP_INDEX.name: SWAP_INDEX
SWAP_INDEX.comment: The positioning of the swap within a transaction. The first swap will index at 0. A swap transaction may only run through 1 pool, in which case the max index will be 0. A multi-pool swap that runs through 2 pools, for example, will have 2 records with swap index 0 and 1.

TRADER.name: TRADER
TRADER.comment: The account address of the trader performing the swap action.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: Protocol on which the event (dex swap, nft trade) occurred, where able to determine.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The source address of the token out in the swap record. If this is the first token withdraw in a swap, then the address is likely to be the trader. This could also be the address of a pool if this swap index is later in the transaction.

TOKEN_OUT.name: TOKEN_OUT
TOKEN_OUT.comment:
The smart contract address for the token that is being swapped out of the traders wallet.
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

TOKEN_OUT_SYMBOL.name: TOKEN_OUT_SYMBOL
TOKEN_OUT_SYMBOL.comment: Short-name symbol for the crypto token, e.g. BLT.

AMOUNT_OUT.name: AMOUNT_OUT
AMOUNT_OUT.comment:
The amount of the token being swapped that the trader is sending to the pool (selling).
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

AMOUNT_OUT_USD.name: AMOUNT_OUT_USD
AMOUNT_OUT_USD.comment: Amount denominated in USD, where pricing data is available.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The destination address of the token in for the swap index. If this is the initial withdraw/deposit event interaction, it is likely to be the pool address for the swap. If this is the final destination for the token, this is likely to be the trader's address.

TOKEN_IN.name: TOKEN_IN
TOKEN_IN.comment:
The smart contract address for the token that is being swapped into the traders wallet.
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

TOKEN_IN_SYMBOL.name: TOKEN_IN_SYMBOL
TOKEN_IN_SYMBOL.comment: Short-name symbol for the crypto token, e.g. BLT.

AMOUNT_IN.name: AMOUNT_IN
AMOUNT_IN.comment:
The amount of the token being swapped that the trader is receiving from the pool (buying).
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

AMOUNT_IN_USD.name: AMOUNT_IN_USD
AMOUNT_IN_USD.comment: Amount denominated in USD, where pricing data is available.

EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID.name: EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID
EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: STREAMLINE_EVENTS
metadata.comment: This table records events from each transaction on the FLOW blockchain.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

EVENT_ID.name: EVENT_ID
EVENT_ID.comment: The id for the event, which is a concatenation of tx_id-event_index.

EVENT_INDEX.name: EVENT_INDEX
EVENT_INDEX.comment: The index of the event within the transaction, i.e. in what order the events occurred.

PAYLOAD.name: PAYLOAD
PAYLOAD.comment: The innermost portion of a transaction and contains the data that uniquely identifies the operations applied by the transaction.

EVENT_CONTRACT.name: EVENT_CONTRACT
EVENT_CONTRACT.comment: The contract called for this event. This is equivalent to the Contract column on Flowscan and is a concatenation of the contract's account address and primary name.

EVENT_TYPE.name: EVENT_TYPE
EVENT_TYPE.comment: The type of method called on the event_contract. In the fact_events table, this represents the method called on a contract. This may also reference the full user-defined event type from the event object. See more on this in the Flow docs: https://developers.flow.com/build/basics/events#user-defined-events.

EVENT_DATA.name: EVENT_DATA
EVENT_DATA.comment: The data passed to the event, in the form of key-value pairs.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Transaction status, if it succeeded or failed.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Time the record was inserted into snowflake

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_ID.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_ID
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_ID.comment:
Partition column used for efficient querying.  
`ROUND(block_number, -5)`

STREAMLINE_EVENT_ID.name: STREAMLINE_EVENT_ID
STREAMLINE_EVENT_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: SWAPS_SINGLE_TRADE_S
metadata.comment: This table records single-pool asset swaps on the Flow blockchain. A single-pool asset swap is one where a liquidity pool exists for the trade such that no routing is required. (i.e. Swapping FUSD to USDT using `A.87f3f233f34b0733.FusdUsdtSwapPair`)

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

SWAP_CONTRACT.name: SWAP_CONTRACT
SWAP_CONTRACT.comment:
The smart contract address of the DEX swap pool.
Note for metapier swaps in the labels table - they all appear to use the same master contract `A.609e10301860b683.PierPair` so the `pool_id` is essential to differentiating what pool is used in the swap route.

TRADER.name: TRADER
TRADER.comment: The account address of the trader performing the swap action.

TOKEN_OUT_AMOUNT.name: TOKEN_OUT_AMOUNT
TOKEN_OUT_AMOUNT.comment:
The amount of the token being swapped that the trader is sending to the pool (selling).
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

TOKEN_OUT_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN_OUT_CONTRACT
TOKEN_OUT_CONTRACT.comment:
The smart contract address for the token that is being swapped out of the traders wallet.
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

TOKEN_IN_AMOUNT.name: TOKEN_IN_AMOUNT
TOKEN_IN_AMOUNT.comment:
The amount of the token being swapped that the trader is receiving from the pool (buying).
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

TOKEN_IN_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN_IN_CONTRACT
TOKEN_IN_CONTRACT.comment:
The smart contract address for the token that is being swapped into the traders wallet.
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

SWAP_SIDE.name: SWAP_SIDE
SWAP_SIDE.comment: The side of the pool that the trader is engaging, per the Trade or Swap event_data.

TOKEN_1_AMOUNT.name: TOKEN_1_AMOUNT
TOKEN_1_AMOUNT.comment: Amount being swapped for token 1 in the swap pair.

TOKEN_2_AMOUNT.name: TOKEN_2_AMOUNT
TOKEN_2_AMOUNT.comment: Amount being swapped for token 2 in the swap pair.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Time the record was inserted into snowflake

SWAPS_SINGLE_TRADE_ID.name: SWAPS_SINGLE_TRADE_ID
SWAPS_SINGLE_TRADE_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: DIM_MOMENT_METADATA
metadata.comment: NFT Moment Metadata scraped from on-chain activity, where available. This should be joinable on sales or mints using the nft_collection (event_contract) and nft id.

NFT_COLLECTION.name: NFT_COLLECTION
NFT_COLLECTION.comment: The contract address or ID for the NFT Collection.

NFT_ID.name: NFT_ID
NFT_ID.comment: The id of the NFT, usually a number.

SERIAL_NUMBER.name: SERIAL_NUMBER
SERIAL_NUMBER.comment: The serial number for the Moment, such as 25, within the collection.

MAX_MINT_SIZE.name: MAX_MINT_SIZE
MAX_MINT_SIZE.comment: The gross NFTs minted for the batch.

PLAY_ID.name: PLAY_ID
PLAY_ID.comment: The numeric ID of the play for the moment. For example, with topshot moment ABC may be a version of Play 123. The play id is 123 and moment metadata is associated with the play, rather than the moment.

SERIES_ID.name: SERIES_ID
SERIES_ID.comment: The numeric ID for the series of moments or plays. This corresponds with the series column found in some metadata tables.

SERIES_NAME.name: SERIES_NAME
SERIES_NAME.comment: The series for this particular set of drops.

SET_ID.name: SET_ID
SET_ID.comment: The numeric ID for the set. This corresponds with the set name found in some metadata columns.

SET_NAME.name: SET_NAME
SET_NAME.comment: Name of the set in which the moment was dropped.

EDITION_ID.name: EDITION_ID
EDITION_ID.comment: The numeric ID for the edition of moments. This is likely a bundle of moment NFTs within the same type of play, differentiated by some trait like rarity.

TIER.name: TIER
TIER.comment: The tiered rarity of the edition.

METADATA.name: METADATA
METADATA.comment: A JSON object containing moment or play metadata.

DIM_MOMENT_METADATA_ID.name: DIM_MOMENT_METADATA_ID
DIM_MOMENT_METADATA_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: NFT_TRANSACTIONS_SECONDARY_MARKET_S
metadata.comment: This table filters all NFT sales that interact with the general purpose contract `A.4eb8a10cb9f87357.NFTStorefront`. Transactions that purchase multiple NFTs in a single transaction are presently excluded from this table!

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

MARKETPLACE.name: MARKETPLACE
MARKETPLACE.comment: Contract address for the marketplace where the transaction occurred. Flow uses a general-purpose contract at `A.4eb8a10cb9f87357.NFTStorefront` for a significant number of sales. This column does not necessarily indicate the website or specific platform on which the sale occurred.

NFT_COLLECTION.name: NFT_COLLECTION
NFT_COLLECTION.comment: The contract address or ID for the NFT Collection.

NFT_ID.name: NFT_ID
NFT_ID.comment: The id of the NFT, usually a number.

CURRENCY.name: CURRENCY
CURRENCY.comment: The contract address refering to the currency used in the transaction. This may or may not relate to a token in the prices table, depending on the marketplace or transaction.

PRICE.name: PRICE
PRICE.comment: The price of the asset, in the currency specified in the table.

SELLER.name: SELLER
SELLER.comment: The party in the transaction selling the asset, this is an account address.

BUYER.name: BUYER
BUYER.comment: The party in the transaction purchasing the asset, this is an account address.

TOKENFLOW.name: TOKENFLOW
TOKENFLOW.comment: An array of events tracking the flow of tokens, in event_index order, for a transaction. This is constructed from the following events within a transaction: `TokensDeposited`, `TokensWithdrawn`, `ForwardedDeposit`.

NUM_STEPS.name: NUM_STEPS
NUM_STEPS.comment: The number of steps (events) taken by tokens within the transaction. This includes only tokenflow steps, and is not the count of total events within the transaction.

STEP_ACTION.name: STEP_ACTION
STEP_ACTION.comment: An array containing the actions taken at index `n` within the tokenflow.

STEP_DATA.name: STEP_DATA
STEP_DATA.comment: An array containing the data passed at index `n` within the tokenflow.

COUNTERPARTIES.name: COUNTERPARTIES
COUNTERPARTIES.comment: An array containing the addresses interacted with during the tokenflow of the transaction. This contains all payers and recipients of tokens within the transaction.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Transaction status, if it succeeded or failed.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Time the record was inserted into snowflake

TRANSACTIONS_SECONDARY_MARKET_ID.name: TRANSACTIONS_SECONDARY_MARKET_ID
TRANSACTIONS_SECONDARY_MARKET_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment:
For information on how to submit a contract for decoding, as well as how ABIs are sourced, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/).
      This model contains decoded event logs for contracts that we have an ABI for. Please note, this table does not include all event logs, only those that we have an ABI for.
      The `decoded_log` column is the easiest place to query decoded data. It is a JSON object, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters.
      You can select from this column using the following sample format, `decoded_log:from::string` or more generally, `decoded_log:<event_param>::datatype`. See below for a full sample query.
      The `full_decoded_logs` column contains the same information, as well as additional fields such as the datatype of the decoded data. You may need to laterally flatten this column to query the data.
            
      Sample query for USDC Transfer events:
      
      ```sql
      select 
      tx_hash,
      block_number,
      contract_address,
      decoded_log:from::string as from_address,
      decoded_log:to::string as to_address,
      decoded_log:value::integer as value
      from ethereum.core.fact_decoded_event_logs
      where contract_address = lower('0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48')
      and block_number between 16400000 and 16405000
      and event_name = 'Transfer'
      limit 50```

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract that the event log was emitted from.

TOPIC_0.name: TOPIC_0
TOPIC_0.comment: The first topic of the event, which is a unique identifier for the event.

TOPIC_1.name: TOPIC_1
TOPIC_1.comment: The second topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_2.name: TOPIC_2
TOPIC_2.comment: The third topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_3.name: TOPIC_3
TOPIC_3.comment: The fourth topic of the event, if applicable.

EVENT_REMOVED.name: EVENT_REMOVED
EVENT_REMOVED.comment: Whether the event has been removed from the transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the contract call that triggered this transaction.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Whether the transaction was successful, returned as a boolean.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event, as defined in the contract ABI.

FULL_DECODED_LOG.name: FULL_DECODED_LOG
FULL_DECODED_LOG.comment: The full decoded log, including the event name, the event parameters, and the data type of the event parameters.

DECODED_LOG.name: DECODED_LOG
DECODED_LOG.comment: The flattened decoded log, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters.

CONTRACT_NAME.name: CONTRACT_NAME
CONTRACT_NAME.comment: The name of the contract, if the contract has a name() function.

EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS_ID.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS_ID
EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: DIM_CONTRACT_LABELS
metadata.comment: This table extracts all contract labels referenced in the events item of a Flow transaction.

EVENT_CONTRACT.name: EVENT_CONTRACT
EVENT_CONTRACT.comment: The contract called for this event. This is equivalent to the Contract column on Flowscan and is a concatenation of the contract's account address and primary name.

CONTRACT_NAME.name: CONTRACT_NAME
CONTRACT_NAME.comment: The primary name of the contract, derived from the full contract address.

ACCOUNT_ADDRESS.name: ACCOUNT_ADDRESS
ACCOUNT_ADDRESS.comment: The account address in 0x form that instantiated the contract called in the event. Note, on the Flow blockchain the account and the contract are separate as the account may have created more than one contract, in rare instances, such as the following FLOW address 0x8624b52f9ddcd04a.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

DIM_CONTRACT_LABELS_ID.name: DIM_CONTRACT_LABELS_ID
DIM_CONTRACT_LABELS_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: BRIDGE_BLOCTO_S
metadata.comment: This table parses transactions where tokens are bridged to or from the Flow network via Blocto Teleport.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

TELEPORT_CONTRACT.name: TELEPORT_CONTRACT
TELEPORT_CONTRACT.comment: The contract address for the bridge used to move tokens to or from Flow.

TOKEN_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN_CONTRACT
TOKEN_CONTRACT.comment: The contract address for a token on the Flow blockchain.

GROSS_AMOUNT.name: GROSS_AMOUNT
GROSS_AMOUNT.comment: The amount of the asset involved in the transaction, before any fees or royalities.

AMOUNT_FEE.name: AMOUNT_FEE
AMOUNT_FEE.comment: The fee taken by the protocol or contract as part of the transaction.

NET_AMOUNT.name: NET_AMOUNT
NET_AMOUNT.comment: The amount of the asset involved in the transaction, net of any fees or royalties.

FLOW_WALLET_ADDRESS.name: FLOW_WALLET_ADDRESS
FLOW_WALLET_ADDRESS.comment: Address of a FLOW wallet related to the transaction.

TELEPORT_DIRECTION.name: TELEPORT_DIRECTION
TELEPORT_DIRECTION.comment: For bridging transactions, inbound refers to tokens bridged to the Flow blockchain, while outbound indicated tokens were sent from Flow to another (i.e. to Ethereum, BSC, etc.).

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: The name of the blockchain for this address or transaction.

BRIDGE.name: BRIDGE
BRIDGE.comment: The name of the bridge or protocol used.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Time the record was inserted into snowflake

has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This convenience table contains emitted event logs for ERC-20 Token Transfers (e.g. `Transfer`: topic_0 = `0xddf252ad1be2c89b69c2b068fc378daa952ba7f163c4a11628f55a4df523b3ef`), including decimal adjusted values, USD values, and other helpful token metadata where available for each transfer event. The contract address is the token transferred, and the raw amount field is the amount of tokens transferred. Note, this table does not contain ERC-721 and ERC-1155 token transfers, instead please use `nft.ez_nft_transfers`. Additionally, this table does not contain transfers of the chain's native asset, instead please use `core.ez_native_transfers`.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transfer.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transfer. This can be a contract address.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract that the event log was emitted from.

TOKEN_STANDARD.name: TOKEN_STANDARD
TOKEN_STANDARD.comment: The type of token transferred in this event, for example `erc721`, `erc1155`, or `erc20`.

NAME.name: NAME
NAME.comment: The name of the deployed contract. Please note this is not necessarily unique.

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: The symbol used to represent this contract. Please note this is not necessarily unique.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The decimals for the token contract.

RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE.name: RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE
RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE.comment: The amount of tokens transferred returned as a string to preserve precision. This value is not decimal adjusted.

RAW_AMOUNT.name: RAW_AMOUNT
RAW_AMOUNT.comment: The amount of tokens transferred. This value is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_PRECISE.name: AMOUNT_PRECISE
AMOUNT_PRECISE.comment: The decimal transformed amount for this token returned as a string to preserve precision. Tokens without a decimal adjustment will be nulled out here.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The decimal transformed amount for this token. Tokens without a decimal adjustment will be nulled out here.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: The amount in US dollars for this transfer at the time of the transfer. Tokens without a decimal adjustment or price will be nulled out here.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the call that triggered this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS_ID.name: EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS_ID
EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_NATIVE_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This convenience table contains all transfers for the chain's native asset, sourced from internal traces (`core.fact_traces`), and includes decimal adjusted and USD values where available. The origin addresses correspond to the to and from addresses in the `core.fact_transactions` table. Note, this table does not contain ERC-721 and ERC-1155 token transfers, instead please use `nft.ez_nft_transfers`.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

TRACE_INDEX.name: TRACE_INDEX
TRACE_INDEX.comment: The index of the trace within the transaction.

TRACE_ADDRESS.name: TRACE_ADDRESS
TRACE_ADDRESS.comment: The trace address for this trace.

TYPE.name: TYPE
TYPE.comment: The type of internal transaction. Common trace types are `CALL`, `DELEGATECALL`, and `STATICCALL`.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transfer.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transfer. This can be a contract address.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The decimal transformed amount for this token. Tokens without a decimal adjustment will be nulled out here.

AMOUNT_PRECISE_RAW.name: AMOUNT_PRECISE_RAW
AMOUNT_PRECISE_RAW.comment: The amount of tokens transferred returned as a string to preserve precision. This value is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_PRECISE.name: AMOUNT_PRECISE
AMOUNT_PRECISE.comment: The decimal transformed amount for this token returned as a string to preserve precision. Tokens without a decimal adjustment will be nulled out here.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: Native asset value transferred, in USD.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the call that triggered this transaction.

EZ_NATIVE_TRANSFERS_ID.name: EZ_NATIVE_TRANSFERS_ID
EZ_NATIVE_TRANSFERS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: STREAMLINE_TRANSACTIONS_FINAL
metadata.comment: This table records all the transactions of the FLOW blockchain.

PENDING_RESULT_RESPONSE.name: PENDING_RESULT_RESPONSE
PENDING_RESULT_RESPONSE.comment: Internal column tracking if the events response has been received yet, to re-sync the tx_id and append events.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

GAS_LIMIT.name: GAS_LIMIT
GAS_LIMIT.comment: Upper gas limit attached to the transaction.

PAYER.name: PAYER
PAYER.comment: Address of the wallet paying for the transaction.

ARGUMENTS.name: ARGUMENTS
ARGUMENTS.comment: The arguments passed into the Cadence script when the transaction was submitted.

ENVELOPE_SIGNATURES.name: ENVELOPE_SIGNATURES
ENVELOPE_SIGNATURES.comment:
A list of signatures generated by the payer role.

https://developers.flow.com/concepts/start-here/transaction-signing#anatomy-of-a-transaction

PAYLOAD_SIGNATURES.name: PAYLOAD_SIGNATURES
PAYLOAD_SIGNATURES.comment:
The authorizing signatures included with the payload in the transaction body.

https://developers.flow.com/concepts/start-here/transaction-signing#payload

PROPOSAL_KEY.name: PROPOSAL_KEY
PROPOSAL_KEY.comment:
Each transaction must declare a proposal key, which can be an account key from any Flow account. The account that owns the proposal key is referred to as the proposer.

A proposal key definition declares the address, key ID, and up-to-date sequence number for the account key.

https://developers.flow.com/concepts/start-here/transaction-signing#proposal-key

SCRIPT.name: SCRIPT
SCRIPT.comment:
The Cadence script executed in this transaction.

https://developers.flow.com/tooling/fcl-js/scripts

EVENTS.name: EVENTS
EVENTS.comment: An array containing all events that occurred in the transaction.

ERROR_MESSAGE.name: ERROR_MESSAGE
ERROR_MESSAGE.comment: The error message, if a transaction has failed.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Transaction status, if it succeeded or failed.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Time the record was inserted into snowflake

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_ID.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_ID
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_ID.comment:
Partition column used for efficient querying.  
`ROUND(block_number, -5)`

STREAMLINE_TRANSACTION_ID.name: STREAMLINE_TRANSACTION_ID
STREAMLINE_TRANSACTION_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

row_count.label: Row Count
row_count.description: An approximate count of rows in this table

clustering_key.label: Clustering Key
clustering_key.description: The key used to cluster this table

bytes.label: Approximate Size
bytes.description: Approximate size of the table as reported by Snowflake

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: FACT_TRACES
metadata.comment: This table contains flattened trace data for internal contract calls. User-defined functions (UDFs) are available to decode hex encoded fields, including `utils.udf_hex_to_int()`.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

TRACE_INDEX.name: TRACE_INDEX
TRACE_INDEX.comment: The index of the trace within the transaction.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transaction.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transaction. This can be a contract address.

INPUT.name: INPUT
INPUT.comment: The input data for this trace.

OUTPUT.name: OUTPUT
OUTPUT.comment: The output data for this trace.

TYPE.name: TYPE
TYPE.comment: The type of internal transaction. Common trace types are `CALL`, `DELEGATECALL`, and `STATICCALL`.

TRACE_ADDRESS.name: TRACE_ADDRESS
TRACE_ADDRESS.comment: The trace address for this trace.

SUB_TRACES.name: SUB_TRACES
SUB_TRACES.comment: The amount of nested sub traces for this trace.

VALUE.name: VALUE
VALUE.comment: The value transacted in the native asset.

VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.name: VALUE_PRECISE_RAW
VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.comment: The precise, unadjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

VALUE_PRECISE.name: VALUE_PRECISE
VALUE_PRECISE.comment: The precise, adjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

VALUE_HEX.name: VALUE_HEX
VALUE_HEX.comment: The value of the transaction in hexadecimal format.

GAS.name: GAS
GAS.comment: The gas supplied for this trace.

GAS_USED.name: GAS_USED
GAS_USED.comment: The gas used for this trace.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this trace. This is not necessarily the from address of the transaction.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this trace. This is not necessarily the to address of the transaction.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the contract call that triggered this transaction.

TRACE_SUCCEEDED.name: TRACE_SUCCEEDED
TRACE_SUCCEEDED.comment: The boolean value representing if the trace succeeded.

ERROR_REASON.name: ERROR_REASON
ERROR_REASON.comment: The reason for the trace failure, if any.

REVERT_REASON.name: REVERT_REASON
REVERT_REASON.comment: The reason for the revert, if available.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Whether the transaction was successful, returned as a boolean.

FACT_TRACES_ID.name: FACT_TRACES_ID
FACT_TRACES_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

row_count.description: An approximate count of rows in this table

metadata.name: VALIDATOR_LABELS
metadata.comment: This table stores all of the Validator Node IDs and the type of validator, per id.

NODE_ID.name: NODE_ID
NODE_ID.comment: The address, known on the Flow blockchain as Node ID, of the validator.

VALIDATOR_TYPE.name: VALIDATOR_TYPE
VALIDATOR_TYPE.comment: The role of the node from one of the following options: collection, consensus, execution, verification, access. [See the Flow documentaion here for more.](https://docs.onflow.org/node-operation/node-roles/).

PROJECT_NAME.name: PROJECT_NAME
PROJECT_NAME.comment:
The overarching project name, related to the address name. For example, Kraken (vs. Kraken Deposit Wallet for address_names).
For validators, this will be the name only if provided as most validators do not provide such a name.

metadata.name: STREAMLINE_TRANSACTIONS
metadata.comment: -| Initial table for the gRPC transactions response, loading data into Snowflake from the external AWS table.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: The block number, corresponds with height.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The id, or hash, of the block.

GAS_LIMIT.name: GAS_LIMIT
GAS_LIMIT.comment: Upper gas limit attached to the transaction.

PAYER.name: PAYER
PAYER.comment: Address of the wallet paying for the transaction.

ARGUMENTS.name: ARGUMENTS
ARGUMENTS.comment: The arguments passed into the Cadence script when the transaction was submitted.

ENVELOPE_SIGNATURES.name: ENVELOPE_SIGNATURES
ENVELOPE_SIGNATURES.comment:
A list of signatures generated by the payer role.

https://developers.flow.com/concepts/start-here/transaction-signing#anatomy-of-a-transaction

PAYLOAD_SIGNATURES.name: PAYLOAD_SIGNATURES
PAYLOAD_SIGNATURES.comment:
The authorizing signatures included with the payload in the transaction body.

https://developers.flow.com/concepts/start-here/transaction-signing#payload

PROPOSAL_KEY.name: PROPOSAL_KEY
PROPOSAL_KEY.comment:
Each transaction must declare a proposal key, which can be an account key from any Flow account. The account that owns the proposal key is referred to as the proposer.

A proposal key definition declares the address, key ID, and up-to-date sequence number for the account key.

https://developers.flow.com/concepts/start-here/transaction-signing#proposal-key

SCRIPT.name: SCRIPT
SCRIPT.comment:
The Cadence script executed in this transaction.

https://developers.flow.com/tooling/fcl-js/scripts

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_ID.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_ID
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_ID.comment:
Partition column used for efficient querying.  
`ROUND(block_number, -5)`

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Time the record was inserted into snowflake

STREAMLINE_TX_ID.name: STREAMLINE_TX_ID
STREAMLINE_TX_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: DIM_SWAP_POOL_LABELS
metadata.comment: This table takes swap pool/pair creation events and models out the relevant info, such as contracts for the token pairs, when it was deployed, and the address of the vault where tokens seem to be held.

SWAP_CONTRACT.name: SWAP_CONTRACT
SWAP_CONTRACT.comment:
The smart contract address of the DEX swap pool.
Note for metapier swaps in the labels table - they all appear to use the same master contract `A.609e10301860b683.PierPair` so the `pool_id` is essential to differentiating what pool is used in the swap route.

DEPLOYMENT_TIMESTAMP.name: DEPLOYMENT_TIMESTAMP
DEPLOYMENT_TIMESTAMP.comment: The block timestamp from the deployment transaction.

TOKEN0_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN0_CONTRACT
TOKEN0_CONTRACT.comment: The contract for token0 in the swap pair. Positioning is determined by the order this token occurs in the Swap Contract creation transaction.

TOKEN1_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN1_CONTRACT
TOKEN1_CONTRACT.comment: The contract for token1 in the swap pair. Positioning is determined by the order this token occurs in the Swap Contract creation transaction.

POOL_ID.name: POOL_ID
POOL_ID.comment: The ID of the swap pool, as designated by the DEX on contract creation.

VAULT_ADDRESS.name: VAULT_ADDRESS
VAULT_ADDRESS.comment: The account address that holds tokens on behalf of the swap contract.

DIM_SWAP_POOL_LABELS_ID.name: DIM_SWAP_POOL_LABELS_ID
DIM_SWAP_POOL_LABELS_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: FACT_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment: This table contains flattened event logs from transactions on this EVM blockchain. Transactions may have multiple events, which are denoted by the event index for a transaction hash. Therefore, this table is unique on the combination of transaction hash and event index. Please see `fact_decoded_event_logs` or `ez_decoded_event_logs` for the decoded event logs.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

TOPIC_0.name: TOPIC_0
TOPIC_0.comment: The first topic of the event, which is a unique identifier for the event.

TOPIC_1.name: TOPIC_1
TOPIC_1.comment: The second topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_2.name: TOPIC_2
TOPIC_2.comment: The third topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_3.name: TOPIC_3
TOPIC_3.comment: The fourth topic of the event, if applicable.

EVENT_REMOVED.name: EVENT_REMOVED
EVENT_REMOVED.comment: Whether the event has been removed from the transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transaction. This can be a contract address.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the contract call that triggered this transaction.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Whether the transaction was successful, returned as a boolean.

FACT_EVENT_LOGS_ID.name: FACT_EVENT_LOGS_ID
FACT_EVENT_LOGS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

clustering_key.description: The key used to cluster this table

metadata.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS
metadata.comment:
This table contains transaction level data for this EVM blockchain. Each transaction will have a unique transaction hash, along with transaction fees and a value transferred in the native asset when applicable. Transactions may be native asset transfers or interactions with contract addresses. For more information, please see [The Ethereum Organization - Transactions](https://ethereum.org/en/developers/docs/transactions/)

Below are the specific native tokens that correspond to each EVM chain:

| Status     | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| ETHEREUM   | ETH         |
| BINANCE    | BNB         |
| POLYGON    | POL         |
| AVALANCHE  | AVAX        |
| ARBITRUM   | ETH         |
| OPTIMISM   | ETH         |
| GNOSIS     | xDAI        |
| KAIA       | KLAY        |
| SEI        | SEI         |
| CORE       | CORE        |

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transaction.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transaction. This can be a contract address.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the call that triggered this transaction.

VALUE.name: VALUE
VALUE.comment: The value transacted in the native asset.

VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.name: VALUE_PRECISE_RAW
VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.comment: The precise, unadjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

VALUE_PRECISE.name: VALUE_PRECISE
VALUE_PRECISE.comment: The precise, adjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

TX_FEE.name: TX_FEE
TX_FEE.comment: Amount paid to validate the transaction in the native asset.

TX_FEE_PRECISE.name: TX_FEE_PRECISE
TX_FEE_PRECISE.comment: The precise amount of the transaction fee. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Whether the transaction was successful, returned as a boolean.

NONCE.name: NONCE
NONCE.comment: The number of transactions sent from a given address.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

INPUT_DATA.name: INPUT_DATA
INPUT_DATA.comment: This column contains additional data for this transaction, and is commonly used as part of a contract interaction or as a message to the recipient.

GAS_PRICE.name: GAS_PRICE
GAS_PRICE.comment: Cost per unit of gas in Gwei.

GAS_LIMIT.name: GAS_LIMIT
GAS_LIMIT.comment: Maximum amount of gas allocated for the transaction.

CUMULATIVE_GAS_USED.name: CUMULATIVE_GAS_USED
CUMULATIVE_GAS_USED.comment: The total amount of gas used when this transaction was executed in the block.

EFFECTIVE_GAS_PRICE.name: EFFECTIVE_GAS_PRICE
EFFECTIVE_GAS_PRICE.comment: The total base charge plus tip paid for each unit of gas, in Gwei.

MAX_FEE_PER_GAS.name: MAX_FEE_PER_GAS
MAX_FEE_PER_GAS.comment: The maximum fee per gas of the transaction, in Gwei.

MAX_PRIORITY_FEE_PER_GAS.name: MAX_PRIORITY_FEE_PER_GAS
MAX_PRIORITY_FEE_PER_GAS.comment: The maximum priority fee per gas of the transaction, in Gwei.

Y_PARITY.name: Y_PARITY
Y_PARITY.comment: The y parity for the transaction.

ACCESS_LIST.name: ACCESS_LIST
ACCESS_LIST.comment: The access list for the transaction.

R.name: R
R.comment: The r value of the transaction signature.

S.name: S
S.comment: The s value of the transaction signature.

V.name: V
V.comment: The v value of the transaction signature.

FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID
FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: NFT_TOPSHOT_PACK_SALES_S
metadata.comment: TopShot pack sales

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

MARKETPLACE.name: MARKETPLACE
MARKETPLACE.comment: Contract address for the marketplace where the transaction occurred. Flow uses a general-purpose contract at `A.4eb8a10cb9f87357.NFTStorefront` for a significant number of sales. This column does not necessarily indicate the website or specific platform on which the sale occurred.

NFT_COLLECTION.name: NFT_COLLECTION
NFT_COLLECTION.comment: The contract address or ID for the NFT Collection.

NFT_ID.name: NFT_ID
NFT_ID.comment: The id of the NFT, usually a number.

BUYER.name: BUYER
BUYER.comment: The party in the transaction purchasing the asset, this is an account address.

SELLER.name: SELLER
SELLER.comment: The party in the transaction selling the asset, this is an account address.

PRICE.name: PRICE
PRICE.comment: The price of the asset, in the currency specified in the table.

CURRENCY.name: CURRENCY
CURRENCY.comment: The contract address refering to the currency used in the transaction. This may or may not relate to a token in the prices table, depending on the marketplace or transaction.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Transaction status, if it succeeded or failed.

TOKENFLOW.name: TOKENFLOW
TOKENFLOW.comment: An array of events tracking the flow of tokens, in event_index order, for a transaction. This is constructed from the following events within a transaction: `TokensDeposited`, `TokensWithdrawn`, `ForwardedDeposit`.

COUNTERPARTIES.name: COUNTERPARTIES
COUNTERPARTIES.comment: An array containing the addresses interacted with during the tokenflow of the transaction. This contains all payers and recipients of tokens within the transaction.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Time the record was inserted into snowflake

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

VALIDATOR_TYPE.name: VALIDATOR_TYPE

PROJECT_NAME.name: PROJECT_NAME

The overarching project name, related to the address name. For example, Kraken (vs. Kraken Deposit Wallet for address_names).
For validators, this will be the name only if provided as most validators do not provide such a name.

metadata.name: DIM_ALLDAY_METADATA
metadata.comment: Data for NFL AllDay Moments, including player, team, stats and more. This is produced via API and may differ in structure from metadata available on-chain in the `dim_moment_metadata` table.

NFT_ID.name: NFT_ID
NFT_ID.comment: The id of the NFT, usually a number.

NFT_COLLECTION.name: NFT_COLLECTION
NFT_COLLECTION.comment: The contract address or ID for the NFT Collection.

NFLALLDAY_ID.name: NFLALLDAY_ID
NFLALLDAY_ID.comment: The ID used by NFL AllDay. This can be pasted into the URL https://nflallday.com/moments/{nflallday_id} to view the moment on the marketplace.

SERIAL_NUMBER.name: SERIAL_NUMBER
SERIAL_NUMBER.comment: The serial number for the Moment, such as 25, within the collection.

TOTAL_CIRCULATION.name: TOTAL_CIRCULATION
TOTAL_CIRCULATION.comment: The total number of moments minted for the particular play.

MOMENT_DESCRIPTION.name: MOMENT_DESCRIPTION
MOMENT_DESCRIPTION.comment: Long-form description of the play, from NBA TopShot.

PLAYER.name: PLAYER
PLAYER.comment: The player who is the focus of the moment.

TEAM.name: TEAM
TEAM.comment: The team that the player from the moment is on, at the time of the moment.

SEASON.name: SEASON
SEASON.comment: The season during which the moment occurred.

WEEK.name: WEEK
WEEK.comment: The week of the NFL season during which the game occurred.

CLASSIFICATION.name: CLASSIFICATION
CLASSIFICATION.comment: The broad category of play that is shown in the moment.

PLAY_TYPE.name: PLAY_TYPE
PLAY_TYPE.comment: The more granular (than play_category) type of play that is shown in the moment.

SERIES.name: SERIES
SERIES.comment: The series for this particular set of drops.

SET_NAME.name: SET_NAME
SET_NAME.comment: Name of the set in which the moment was dropped.

VIDEO_URLS.name: VIDEO_URLS
VIDEO_URLS.comment: An array containing links to the moments.

MOMENT_STATS_FULL.name: MOMENT_STATS_FULL
MOMENT_STATS_FULL.comment: A dictionary of the full moment stats.

DIM_ALLDAY_METADATA_ID.name: DIM_ALLDAY_METADATA_ID
DIM_ALLDAY_METADATA_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: STAKING_ACTIONS
metadata.comment: This table provides transaction-level info on FLOW staking activities.

EVENT_INDEX.name: EVENT_INDEX
EVENT_INDEX.comment: The index of the event within the transaction, i.e. in what order the events occurred.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Transaction status, if it succeeded or failed.

DELEGATOR.name: DELEGATOR
DELEGATOR.comment: The Flow account address for the wallet delegating to a node. This is derived from the first authorizer on the transaction and may be inaccurate if the transaction was signed by an EOA. Refer to delegator_id as the unique identifier for the delegator, taken directly from the emitted event data.

ACTION.name: ACTION
ACTION.comment: The action occurring in this record, as derived from `event_type` in the transaction.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The amount of the asset involved in the transaction.

NODE_ID.name: NODE_ID
NODE_ID.comment: The address, known on the Flow blockchain as Node ID, of the validator.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Time the record was inserted into snowflake

STAKING_ACTIONS_ID.name: STAKING_ACTIONS_ID

metadata.name: EZ_ASSET_METADATA
metadata.comment: A convenience table holding prioritized asset metadata and other relevant details pertaining to each token_address and native asset. This data set is highly curated and contains metadata for one unique asset per blockchain.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The specific address representing the asset on a specific platform. This will be NULL if referring to a native asset.

ASSET_ID.name: ASSET_ID
ASSET_ID.comment: The unique identifier representing the asset.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The number of decimals for the asset. May be NULL.

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: The Blockchain, Network, or Platform for this asset.

IS_DEPRECATED.name: IS_DEPRECATED
IS_DEPRECATED.comment: A flag indicating if the asset is deprecated or no longer supported by the provider.

IS_NATIVE.name: IS_NATIVE
IS_NATIVE.comment: A flag indicating assets native to the respective blockchain.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

EZ_ASSET_METADATA_ID.name: EZ_ASSET_METADATA_ID
EZ_ASSET_METADATA_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

metadata.name: EZ_NFT_SALES
metadata.comment: This ez_ view is the same data table as the prior `fact_nft_sales` view. NFT market sales on the Flow blockchain. Currently supported in this ez_ view are transactions that interact with the primary NFT Marketplace contract (), the Fabricant NFT Marketplace contract, and NBA TopShot primary market sales. More markets can and will be added.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

MARKETPLACE.name: MARKETPLACE
MARKETPLACE.comment: Contract address for the marketplace where the transaction occurred. Flow uses a general-purpose contract at `A.4eb8a10cb9f87357.NFTStorefront` for a significant number of sales. This column does not necessarily indicate the website or specific platform on which the sale occurred.

NFT_COLLECTION.name: NFT_COLLECTION
NFT_COLLECTION.comment: The contract address or ID for the NFT Collection.

NFT_ID.name: NFT_ID
NFT_ID.comment: The id of the NFT, usually a number.

BUYER.name: BUYER
BUYER.comment: The party in the transaction purchasing the asset, this is an account address.

SELLER.name: SELLER
SELLER.comment: The party in the transaction selling the asset, this is an account address.

PRICE.name: PRICE
PRICE.comment: The price of the asset, in the currency specified in the table.

CURRENCY.name: CURRENCY
CURRENCY.comment: The contract address refering to the currency used in the transaction. This may or may not relate to a token in the prices table, depending on the marketplace or transaction.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Transaction status, if it succeeded or failed.

TOKENFLOW.name: TOKENFLOW
TOKENFLOW.comment: An array of events tracking the flow of tokens, in event_index order, for a transaction. This is constructed from the following events within a transaction: `TokensDeposited`, `TokensWithdrawn`, `ForwardedDeposit`.

COUNTERPARTIES.name: COUNTERPARTIES
COUNTERPARTIES.comment: An array containing the addresses interacted with during the tokenflow of the transaction. This contains all payers and recipients of tokens within the transaction.

EZ_NFT_SALES_ID.name: EZ_NFT_SALES_ID
EZ_NFT_SALES_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: DIM_LABELS
metadata.comment: The labels table is a store of one-to-one address identifiers, or an address name. Labels are broken out into a "type" (such as cex, dex, dapp, games, etc.) and a "subtype" (ex: contract_deployer, hot_wallet, token_contract, etc.) in order to help classify each address name into similar groups. Our labels are sourced from many different places, but can primarily be grouped into two categories: automatic and manual. Automatic labels are continuously labeled based on certain criteria, such as a known contract deploying another contract, behavior-based algorithms for finding deposit wallets, and consistent data pulls of custom protocol APIs. Manual labels are done periodically to find addresses that cannot be found programmatically such as finding new protocol addresses, centralized exchange hot wallets, or trending addresses. Labels can also be added by our community by using our add-a-label tool (https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/add-a-label/) or on-chain with near (https://near.social/lord1.near/widget/Form) and are reviewed by our labels team. A label can be removed by our labels team if it is found to be incorrect or no longer relevant; this generally will only happen for mislabeled deposit wallets.

CREATOR.name: CREATOR
CREATOR.comment: The name of the creator of the label.

ADDRESS.name: ADDRESS
ADDRESS.comment: Address that the label is for. This is the field that should be used to join other tables with labels.

ADDRESS_NAME.name: ADDRESS_NAME
ADDRESS_NAME.comment: The most granular label for this address.

LABEL_TYPE.name: LABEL_TYPE
LABEL_TYPE.comment: A high-level category describing the address's main function or ownership.

LABEL_SUBTYPE.name: LABEL_SUBTYPE
LABEL_SUBTYPE.comment: A sub-category nested within label type providing further detail.

DIM_LABELS_ID.name: DIM_LABELS_ID
DIM_LABELS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

row_count.label: Row Count
row_count.description: An approximate count of rows in this table

clustering_key.description: The key used to cluster this table

metadata.name: DIM_LABELS
metadata.comment: The labels table is a store of one-to-one address identifiers, or an address name. Labels are broken out into a "type" (such as cex, dex, dapp, games, etc.) and a "subtype" (ex: contract_deployer, hot_wallet, token_contract, etc.) in order to help classify each address name into similar groups. Our labels are sourced from many different places, but can primarily be grouped into two categories: automatic and manual. Automatic labels are continuously labeled based on certain criteria, such as a known contract deploying another contract, behavior based algorithms for finding deposit wallets, and consistent data pulls of custom protocol APIs. Manual labels are done periodically to find addresses that cannot be found programmatically such as finding new protocol addresses, centralized exchange hot wallets, or trending addresses. Labels can also be added by our community by using our add-a-label tool (https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/add-a-label/) or on-chain with near (https://near.social/lord1.near/widget/Form) and are reviewed by our labels team.
