metadata.name: NFT_TOPSHOT_METADATA
metadata.comment: Data for TopShot Moments, including player, team, stats and more.

NFT_ID.name: NFT_ID
NFT_ID.comment: The id of the NFT, usually a number.

NFT_COLLECTION.name: NFT_COLLECTION
NFT_COLLECTION.comment: The contract address or ID for the NFT Collection.

NBATOPSHOT_ID.name: NBATOPSHOT_ID
NBATOPSHOT_ID.comment: The ID used by NBA TopShot. This can be pasted into the URL https://nbatopshot.com/moment/{nbatopshot_id} to view the moment on the marketplace.

SERIAL_NUMBER.name: SERIAL_NUMBER
SERIAL_NUMBER.comment: The serial number for the Moment, such as 25, within the collection.

TOTAL_CIRCULATION.name: TOTAL_CIRCULATION
TOTAL_CIRCULATION.comment: The total number of moments minted for the particular play.

MOMENT_DESCRIPTION.name: MOMENT_DESCRIPTION
MOMENT_DESCRIPTION.comment: Long-form description of the play, from NBA TopShot.

PLAYER.name: PLAYER
PLAYER.comment: The player who is the focus of the moment.

TEAM.name: TEAM
TEAM.comment: The team that the player from the moment is on, at the time of the moment.

SEASON.name: SEASON
SEASON.comment: The season during which the moment occurred.

PLAY_CATEGORY.name: PLAY_CATEGORY
PLAY_CATEGORY.comment: The broad category of play that is shown in the moment.

PLAY_TYPE.name: PLAY_TYPE
PLAY_TYPE.comment: The more granular (than play_category) type of play that is shown in the moment.

SET_NAME.name: SET_NAME
SET_NAME.comment: Name of the set in which the moment was dropped.

SET_SERIES_NUMBER.name: SET_SERIES_NUMBER
SET_SERIES_NUMBER.comment: The series number for this particular set of drops, within the set_name.

VIDEO_URLS.name: VIDEO_URLS
VIDEO_URLS.comment: An array containing links to the moments.

MOMENT_STATS_FULL.name: MOMENT_STATS_FULL
MOMENT_STATS_FULL.comment: A dictionary of the full moment stats.

PLAYER_STATS_GAME.name: PLAYER_STATS_GAME
PLAYER_STATS_GAME.comment: The statline for the player in the Moment, from the game highlighted.

PLAYER_STATS_SEASON_TO_DATE.name: PLAYER_STATS_SEASON_TO_DATE
PLAYER_STATS_SEASON_TO_DATE.comment: The season stats for the player as of the game featured in the moment.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Time the record was inserted into snowflake

NFT_MOMENT_METADATA_TOPSHOT_ID.name: NFT_MOMENT_METADATA_TOPSHOT_ID
NFT_MOMENT_METADATA_TOPSHOT_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: LABELS_POOLS_METAPIER_S
metadata.comment: Looks for new PoolCreated events from the Metapier factory and recordes info.

DEPLOYMENT_TIMESTAMP.name: DEPLOYMENT_TIMESTAMP
DEPLOYMENT_TIMESTAMP.comment: The block timestamp from the deployment transaction.

TOKEN0_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN0_CONTRACT
TOKEN0_CONTRACT.comment: The contract for token0 in the swap pair. Positioning is determined by the order this token occurs in the Swap Contract creation transaction.

TOKEN1_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN1_CONTRACT
TOKEN1_CONTRACT.comment: The contract for token1 in the swap pair. Positioning is determined by the order this token occurs in the Swap Contract creation transaction.

POOL_ID.name: POOL_ID
POOL_ID.comment: The ID of the swap pool, as designated by the DEX on contract creation.

VAULT_ADDRESS.name: VAULT_ADDRESS
VAULT_ADDRESS.comment: The account address that holds tokens on behalf of the swap contract.

SWAP_CONTRACT.name: SWAP_CONTRACT
SWAP_CONTRACT.comment:
The smart contract address of the DEX swap pool.
Note for metapier swaps in the labels table - they all appear to use the same master contract `A.609e10301860b683.PierPair` so the `pool_id` is essential to differentiating what pool is used in the swap route.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Time the record was inserted into snowflake

LABELS_POOLS_METAPIER_ID.name: LABELS_POOLS_METAPIER_ID
LABELS_POOLS_METAPIER_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT

SWAP_CONTRACT.comment:
The smart contract address of the DEX swap pool.
Note for metapier swaps in the labels table - they all appear to use the same master contract `A.609e10301860b683.PierPair` so the `pool_id` is essential to differentiating what pool is used in the swap route.

SWAP_INDEX.name: SWAP_INDEX
SWAP_INDEX.comment: The positioning of the swap within a transaction. The first swap will index at 0. A swap transaction may only run through 1 pool, in which case the max index will be 0. A multi-pool swap that runs through 2 pools, for example, will have 2 records with swap index 0 and 1.

TRADER.name: TRADER
TRADER.comment: The account address of the trader performing the swap action.

TOKEN_OUT_SOURCE.name: TOKEN_OUT_SOURCE
TOKEN_OUT_SOURCE.comment: The source address of the token out in the swap record. If this is the first token withdraw in a swap, then the address is likely to be the trader. This could also be the address of a pool if this swap index is later in the transaction.

TOKEN_OUT_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN_OUT_CONTRACT
TOKEN_OUT_CONTRACT.comment:
The smart contract address for the token that is being swapped out of the traders wallet.
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

TOKEN_OUT_AMOUNT.name: TOKEN_OUT_AMOUNT
TOKEN_OUT_AMOUNT.comment:
The amount of the token being swapped that the trader is sending to the pool (selling).
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

TOKEN_IN_DESTINATION.name: TOKEN_IN_DESTINATION
TOKEN_IN_DESTINATION.comment: The destination address of the token in for the swap index. If this is the initial withdraw/deposit event interaction, it is likely to be the pool address for the swap. If this is the final destination for the token, this is likely to be the trader's address.

TOKEN_IN_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN_IN_CONTRACT
TOKEN_IN_CONTRACT.comment:
The smart contract address for the token that is being swapped into the traders wallet.
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

TOKEN_IN_AMOUNT.name: TOKEN_IN_AMOUNT
TOKEN_IN_AMOUNT.comment:
The amount of the token being swapped that the trader is receiving from the pool (buying).
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

POOL_ID.name: POOL_ID

VAULT_ADDRESS.name: VAULT_ADDRESS

TOKEN0_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN0_CONTRACT
TOKEN0_CONTRACT.comment: The contract for token0 in the swap pair. Positioning is determined by the order this token occurs in the Swap Contract creation transaction.

TOKEN1_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN1_CONTRACT
TOKEN1_CONTRACT.comment: The contract for token1 in the swap pair. Positioning is determined by the order this token occurs in the Swap Contract creation transaction.

SWAP_CONTRACT.name: SWAP_CONTRACT

The smart contract address of the DEX swap pool.
Note for metapier swaps in the labels table - they all appear to use the same master contract `A.609e10301860b683.PierPair` so the `pool_id` is essential to differentiating what pool is used in the swap route.

metadata.name: EZ_STAKING_ACTIONS
metadata.comment: This table provides transaction-level info on FLOW staking activities.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Transaction status, if it succeeded or failed.

DELEGATOR.name: DELEGATOR
DELEGATOR.comment: The Flow account address for the wallet delegating to a node. This is derived from the first authorizer on the transaction and may be inaccurate if the transaction was signed by an EOA. Refer to delegator_id as the unique identifier for the delegator, taken directly from the emitted event data.

DELEGATOR_ID.name: DELEGATOR_ID
DELEGATOR_ID.comment: The Flow account address for the wallet delegating to a node. This is derived from the first authorizer on the transaction and may be inaccurate if the transaction was signed by an EOA. Refer to delegator_id as the unique identifier for the delegator, taken directly from the emitted event data.

ACTION.name: ACTION
ACTION.comment: The action occurring in this record, as derived from `event_type` in the transaction.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The amount of the asset involved in the transaction.

NODE_ID.name: NODE_ID
NODE_ID.comment: The address, known on the Flow blockchain as Node ID, of the validator.

EZ_STAKING_ACTIONS_ID.name: EZ_STAKING_ACTIONS_ID
EZ_STAKING_ACTIONS_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY
metadata.comment: A convenience table that aggregates block and transaction related metrics using various aggregate functions such as SUM, COUNT, MIN and MAX from the fact_transactions table, on an hourly basis. Stats for the current hour will be updated as new data arrives.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP_HOUR.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP_HOUR
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP_HOUR.comment: The hour of the timestamp of the block.

BLOCK_NUMBER_MIN.name: BLOCK_NUMBER_MIN
BLOCK_NUMBER_MIN.comment: The minimum block number in the hour.

BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX.name: BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX
BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX.comment: The maximum block number in the hour.

BLOCK_COUNT.name: BLOCK_COUNT
BLOCK_COUNT.comment: The number of blocks in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT
TRANSACTION_COUNT.comment: The number of transactions in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS
TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS.comment: The number of successful transactions in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED
TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED.comment: The number of failed transactions in the hour.

UNIQUE_FROM_COUNT.name: UNIQUE_FROM_COUNT
UNIQUE_FROM_COUNT.comment: The number of unique proposer from addresses in the hour.

TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE.name: TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE
TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE.comment: The sum of all fees in the hour, in the native fee currency.

TOTAL_FEES_USD.name: TOTAL_FEES_USD
TOTAL_FEES_USD.comment: The sum of all fees in the hour, in USD.

EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID.name: EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID
EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: LABELS
metadata.comment: This table provides labels for addresses on the Flow blockchain.

_INGESTED_AT.name: _INGESTED_AT
_INGESTED_AT.comment: When the record was ingested by the chainwalker.

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: The name of the blockchain for this address or transaction.

ADDRESS.name: ADDRESS
ADDRESS.comment: The on-chain address. See more in the Flow docs on accounts and addresses: https://developers.flow.com/build/basics/accounts and https://developers.flow.com/evm/accounts

CREATOR.name: CREATOR
CREATOR.comment: The source of the labeling information for the address.

LABEL_TYPE.name: LABEL_TYPE
LABEL_TYPE.comment: Predominant label categorization, such as CEX, Operator, NFT, etc.

LABEL_SUBTYPE.name: LABEL_SUBTYPE
LABEL_SUBTYPE.comment: The type of address, within the higher order label_type, such as hot wallet, deposit wallet, validator, etc.

ADDRESS_NAME.name: ADDRESS_NAME
ADDRESS_NAME.comment: The name for a specific address, like Kraken or Huobi for CEX, or consensus vs verification for validator.

PROJECT_NAME.name: PROJECT_NAME
PROJECT_NAME.comment:
The overarching project name, related to the address name. For example, Kraken (vs. Kraken Deposit Wallet for address_names).
For validators, this will be the name only if provided as most validators do not provide such a name.

LABELS_ID.name: LABELS_ID
LABELS_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: NFT_MOMENT_METADATA_FINAL_S
metadata.comment: Cleaned PlayCreated events.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

EVENT_CONTRACT.name: EVENT_CONTRACT
EVENT_CONTRACT.comment: The contract called for this event. This is equivalent to the Contract column on Flowscan and is a concatenation of the contract's account address and primary name.

NFT_ID.name: NFT_ID
NFT_ID.comment: The id of the NFT, usually a number.

SERIAL_NUMBER.name: SERIAL_NUMBER
SERIAL_NUMBER.comment: The serial number for the Moment, such as 25, within the collection.

MAX_MINT_SIZE.name: MAX_MINT_SIZE
MAX_MINT_SIZE.comment: The gross NFTs minted for the batch.

PLAY_ID.name: PLAY_ID
PLAY_ID.comment: The numeric ID of the play for the moment. For example, with topshot moment ABC may be a version of Play 123. The play id is 123 and moment metadata is associated with the play, rather than the moment.

SERIES_ID.name: SERIES_ID
SERIES_ID.comment: The numeric ID for the series of moments or plays. This corresponds with the series column found in some metadata tables.

SERIES_NAME.name: SERIES_NAME
SERIES_NAME.comment: The series for this particular set of drops.

SET_ID.name: SET_ID
SET_ID.comment: The numeric ID for the set. This corresponds with the set name found in some metadata columns.

SET_NAME.name: SET_NAME
SET_NAME.comment: Name of the set in which the moment was dropped.

EDITION_ID.name: EDITION_ID
EDITION_ID.comment: The numeric ID for the edition of moments. This is likely a bundle of moment NFTs within the same type of play, differentiated by some trait like rarity.

TIER.name: TIER
TIER.comment: The tiered rarity of the edition.

METADATA.name: METADATA
METADATA.comment: A JSON object containing moment or play metadata.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Time the record was inserted into snowflake

NFT_MOMENT_METADATA_ID.name: NFT_MOMENT_METADATA_ID
NFT_MOMENT_METADATA_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

row_count.label: Row Count
row_count.description: An approximate count of rows in this table

clustering_key.label: Clustering Key
clustering_key.description: The key used to cluster this table

bytes.description: Approximate size of the table as reported by Snowflake

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY
metadata.comment: This table parses transactions where tokens are bridged to or from the Flow network using Blocto teleport or the Celer bridge.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

BRIDGE_ADDRESS.name: BRIDGE_ADDRESS
BRIDGE_ADDRESS.comment: The contract address for the bridge used to move tokens to or from Flow.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The contract address for a token on the Flow blockchain.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The amount of the asset involved in the transaction.

AMOUNT_FEE.name: AMOUNT_FEE
AMOUNT_FEE.comment: The fee taken by the protocol or contract as part of the transaction.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: Amount denominated in USD, where pricing data is available.

AMOUNT_FEE_USD.name: AMOUNT_FEE_USD
AMOUNT_FEE_USD.comment: Amount denominated in USD, where pricing data is available.

SOURCE_ADDRESS.name: SOURCE_ADDRESS
SOURCE_ADDRESS.comment: Address of a FLOW wallet related to the transaction.

DESTINATION_ADDRESS.name: DESTINATION_ADDRESS
DESTINATION_ADDRESS.comment: Address of a FLOW wallet related to the transaction.

SOURCE_CHAIN.name: SOURCE_CHAIN
SOURCE_CHAIN.comment: The name of the blockchain for this address or transaction.

DESTINATION_CHAIN.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN
DESTINATION_CHAIN.comment: The name of the blockchain for this address or transaction.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The name of the bridge or protocol used.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: STREAMLINE_BLOCKS
metadata.comment: -| Initial table for the gRPC blocks response, loading data into Snowflake from the external AWS table.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: The block number, corresponds with height.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

COLLECTION_COUNT.name: COLLECTION_COUNT
COLLECTION_COUNT.comment: The number of collections included in a blocks.

PARENT_ID.name: PARENT_ID
PARENT_ID.comment: The block hash for the parent block.

SIGNATURES.name: SIGNATURES
SIGNATURES.comment: An array of signatures contained in the block header.

COLLECTION_GUARANTEES.name: COLLECTION_GUARANTEES
COLLECTION_GUARANTEES.comment:
A signed attestation that specifies the collection nodes that have guaranteed to store and respond to queries about a collection.

https://developers.flow.com/concepts/nodes/access-api#collection-guarantee

BLOCK_SEALS.name: BLOCK_SEALS
BLOCK_SEALS.comment:
A block seal is an attestation that the execution result of a specific block has been verified and approved by a quorum of verification nodes.

https://developers.flow.com/concepts/nodes/access-api#block-seal

COLLECTION_COUNT_AGG.name: COLLECTION_COUNT_AGG
COLLECTION_COUNT_AGG.comment: Collection count from the tx_count aggregation, used to check against the collection_count in the block header.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_ID.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_ID
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_ID.comment:
Partition column used for efficient querying.  
`ROUND(block_number, -5)`

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Time the record was inserted into snowflake

BLOCKS_ID.name: BLOCKS_ID
BLOCKS_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: NFT_TOPSHOT_SALES_S
metadata.comment: TopShot direct market sales on the Flow blockchain.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

MARKETPLACE.name: MARKETPLACE
MARKETPLACE.comment: Contract address for the marketplace where the transaction occurred. Flow uses a general-purpose contract at `A.4eb8a10cb9f87357.NFTStorefront` for a significant number of sales. This column does not necessarily indicate the website or specific platform on which the sale occurred.

NFT_COLLECTION.name: NFT_COLLECTION
NFT_COLLECTION.comment: The contract address or ID for the NFT Collection.

NFT_ID.name: NFT_ID
NFT_ID.comment: The id of the NFT, usually a number.

BUYER.name: BUYER
BUYER.comment: The party in the transaction purchasing the asset, this is an account address.

SELLER.name: SELLER
SELLER.comment: The party in the transaction selling the asset, this is an account address.

PRICE.name: PRICE
PRICE.comment: The price of the asset, in the currency specified in the table.

CURRENCY.name: CURRENCY
CURRENCY.comment: The contract address refering to the currency used in the transaction. This may or may not relate to a token in the prices table, depending on the marketplace or transaction.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Transaction status, if it succeeded or failed.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Time the record was inserted into snowflake

TOKENFLOW.name: TOKENFLOW
TOKENFLOW.comment: An array of events tracking the flow of tokens, in event_index order, for a transaction. This is constructed from the following events within a transaction: `TokensDeposited`, `TokensWithdrawn`, `ForwardedDeposit`.

COUNTERPARTIES.name: COUNTERPARTIES
COUNTERPARTIES.comment: An array containing the addresses interacted with during the tokenflow of the transaction. This contains all payers and recipients of tokens within the transaction.

NFT_TOPSHOT_SALES_ID.name: NFT_TOPSHOT_SALES_ID
NFT_TOPSHOT_SALES_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: DIM_TOPSHOT_METADATA
metadata.comment: Data for TopShot Moments, including player, team, stats and more. This is produced via API and may differ in structure from metadata available on-chain in the `dim_moment_metadata` table.

NFT_ID.name: NFT_ID
NFT_ID.comment: The id of the NFT, usually a number.

NFT_COLLECTION.name: NFT_COLLECTION
NFT_COLLECTION.comment: The contract address or ID for the NFT Collection.

NBATOPSHOT_ID.name: NBATOPSHOT_ID
NBATOPSHOT_ID.comment: The ID used by NBA TopShot. This can be pasted into the URL https://nbatopshot.com/moment/{nbatopshot_id} to view the moment on the marketplace.

SERIAL_NUMBER.name: SERIAL_NUMBER
SERIAL_NUMBER.comment: The serial number for the Moment, such as 25, within the collection.

TOTAL_CIRCULATION.name: TOTAL_CIRCULATION
TOTAL_CIRCULATION.comment: The total number of moments minted for the particular play.

MOMENT_DESCRIPTION.name: MOMENT_DESCRIPTION
MOMENT_DESCRIPTION.comment: Long-form description of the play, from NBA TopShot.

PLAYER.name: PLAYER
PLAYER.comment: The player who is the focus of the moment.

TEAM.name: TEAM
TEAM.comment: The team that the player from the moment is on, at the time of the moment.

SEASON.name: SEASON
SEASON.comment: The season during which the moment occurred.

PLAY_CATEGORY.name: PLAY_CATEGORY
PLAY_CATEGORY.comment: The broad category of play that is shown in the moment.

PLAY_TYPE.name: PLAY_TYPE
PLAY_TYPE.comment: The more granular (than play_category) type of play that is shown in the moment.

SET_NAME.name: SET_NAME
SET_NAME.comment: Name of the set in which the moment was dropped.

SET_SERIES_NUMBER.name: SET_SERIES_NUMBER
SET_SERIES_NUMBER.comment: The series number for this particular set of drops, within the set_name.

VIDEO_URLS.name: VIDEO_URLS
VIDEO_URLS.comment: An array containing links to the moments.

MOMENT_STATS_FULL.name: MOMENT_STATS_FULL
MOMENT_STATS_FULL.comment: A dictionary of the full moment stats.

PLAYER_STATS_GAME.name: PLAYER_STATS_GAME
PLAYER_STATS_GAME.comment: The statline for the player in the Moment, from the game highlighted.

PLAYER_STATS_SEASON_TO_DATE.name: PLAYER_STATS_SEASON_TO_DATE
PLAYER_STATS_SEASON_TO_DATE.comment: The season stats for the player as of the game featured in the moment.

DIM_TOPSHOT_METADATA_ID.name: DIM_TOPSHOT_METADATA_ID
DIM_TOPSHOT_METADATA_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: CONTRACT_LABELS_S
metadata.comment: This table extracts all contract labels referenced in the events item of a Flow transaction.

EVENT_CONTRACT.name: EVENT_CONTRACT
EVENT_CONTRACT.comment: The contract called for this event. This is equivalent to the Contract column on Flowscan and is a concatenation of the contract's account address and primary name.

CONTRACT_NAME.name: CONTRACT_NAME
CONTRACT_NAME.comment: The primary name of the contract, derived from the full contract address.

ACCOUNT_ADDRESS.name: ACCOUNT_ADDRESS
ACCOUNT_ADDRESS.comment: The account address in 0x form that instantiated the contract called in the event. Note, on the Flow blockchain the account and the contract are separate as the account may have created more than one contract, in rare instances, such as the following FLOW address 0x8624b52f9ddcd04a.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Time the record was inserted into snowflake

EVENT_CONTRACT_ID.name: EVENT_CONTRACT_ID
EVENT_CONTRACT_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP

_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

NETWORK.name: NETWORK

NETWORK_VERSION.name: NETWORK_VERSION

CHAIN_ID.name: CHAIN_ID
CHAIN_ID.comment: The id for the chain of the network on which this block occurred.

TX_COUNT.name: TX_COUNT

PARENT_ID.name: PARENT_ID

metadata.name: BRIDGE_CELER_S
metadata.comment: This table parses transactions where tokens are bridged to or from the Flow network using the Celer cBridge.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

BRIDGE_CONTRACT.name: BRIDGE_CONTRACT
BRIDGE_CONTRACT.comment: The contract address for the bridge used to move tokens to or from Flow.

TOKEN_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN_CONTRACT
TOKEN_CONTRACT.comment: The contract address for a token on the Flow blockchain.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The amount of the asset involved in the transaction.

FLOW_WALLET_ADDRESS.name: FLOW_WALLET_ADDRESS
FLOW_WALLET_ADDRESS.comment: Address of a FLOW wallet related to the transaction.

COUNTERPARTY.name: COUNTERPARTY
COUNTERPARTY.comment:
The alternate address involved in this transaction, if available.  
For bridge transactions, this may be the address on another blockchain.

CHAIN_ID.name: CHAIN_ID
CHAIN_ID.comment: The id for the chain of the network on which this block occurred.

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: The name of the blockchain for this address or transaction.

DIRECTION.name: DIRECTION
DIRECTION.comment: For bridging transactions, inbound refers to tokens bridged to the Flow blockchain, while outbound indicated tokens were sent from Flow to another (i.e. to Ethereum, BSC, etc.).

BRIDGE.name: BRIDGE
BRIDGE.comment: The name of the bridge or protocol used.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Time the record was inserted into snowflake

BRIDGE_CELER_ID.name: BRIDGE_CELER_ID
BRIDGE_CELER_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: DIM_CONTRACT_ABIS
metadata.comment:
This table contains the contract ABIs that we have sourced from Etherscan, the community, or bytecode matched. This table is the source of ABIs used in the `core__ez_decoded_event_logs` table.
We first try to source ABIs from Etherscan. If we cannot find an ABI on Etherscan, we will rely on user submissions. To add a contract to this table, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/).
If we are unable to locate an ABI for a contract from Etherscan or the community, we will try to find an ABI to use by matching the contract bytecode to a known contract bytecode we do have an ABI for.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The unique address of the deployed contract.

ABI.name: ABI
ABI.comment: The JSON ABI for the contract.

ABI_SOURCE.name: ABI_SOURCE
ABI_SOURCE.comment: The source of the ABI. This can be the block explorer, `user_submitted`, or `bytecode_matched`.

BYTECODE.name: BYTECODE
BYTECODE.comment: The deployed bytecode of the contract.

row_count.description: An approximate count of rows in this table

metadata.name: FACT_EVENTS
metadata.comment: This table records events from each transaction on the FLOW blockchain.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Transaction status, if it succeeded or failed.

EVENT_INDEX.name: EVENT_INDEX
EVENT_INDEX.comment: The index of the event within the transaction, i.e. in what order the events occurred.

EVENT_CONTRACT.name: EVENT_CONTRACT
EVENT_CONTRACT.comment: The contract called for this event. This is equivalent to the Contract column on Flowscan and is a concatenation of the contract's account address and primary name.

EVENT_TYPE.name: EVENT_TYPE
EVENT_TYPE.comment: The type of method called on the event_contract. In the fact_events table, this represents the method called on a contract. This may also reference the full user-defined event type from the event object. See more on this in the Flow docs: https://developers.flow.com/build/basics/events#user-defined-events.

EVENT_DATA.name: EVENT_DATA
EVENT_DATA.comment: The data passed to the event, in the form of key-value pairs.

FACT_EVENTS_ID.name: FACT_EVENTS_ID
FACT_EVENTS_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: EZ_PRICES_HOURLY
metadata.comment: A convenience table for determining token prices by address and blockchain, and native asset prices by symbol and blockchain. This data set is highly curated and contains metadata for one price per hour per unique asset and blockchain.

HOUR.name: HOUR
HOUR.comment: Hour that the price was recorded at.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The specific address representing the asset on a specific platform. This will be NULL if referring to a native asset.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The number of decimals for the asset. May be NULL.

PRICE.name: PRICE
PRICE.comment: Closing price of the recorded hour in USD.

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: The Blockchain, Network, or Platform for this asset.

IS_NATIVE.name: IS_NATIVE
IS_NATIVE.comment: A flag indicating assets native to the respective blockchain.

IS_IMPUTED.name: IS_IMPUTED
IS_IMPUTED.comment: A flag indicating if the price was imputed, or derived, from the last arriving record. This is generally used for tokens with low-liquidity or inconsistent reporting.

IS_DEPRECATED.name: IS_DEPRECATED
IS_DEPRECATED.comment: A flag indicating if the asset is deprecated or no longer supported by the provider.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

EZ_PRICES_HOURLY_ID.name: EZ_PRICES_HOURLY_ID
EZ_PRICES_HOURLY_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

metadata.name: SWAPS_AGGREGATOR
metadata.comment: This table records asset swaps on the Flow blockchain parsing the log output of swaps through aggregators.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

SWAP_INDEX.name: SWAP_INDEX
SWAP_INDEX.comment: The positioning of the swap within a transaction. The first swap will index at 0. A swap transaction may only run through 1 pool, in which case the max index will be 0. A multi-pool swap that runs through 2 pools, for example, will have 2 records with swap index 0 and 1.

POOL_ADDRESS.name: POOL_ADDRESS
POOL_ADDRESS.comment:
The smart contract address of the DEX swap pool.
Note for metapier swaps in the labels table - they all appear to use the same master contract `A.609e10301860b683.PierPair` so the `pool_id` is essential to differentiating what pool is used in the swap route.

POOL_SOURCE.name: POOL_SOURCE
POOL_SOURCE.comment: Protocol on which the event (dex swap, nft trade) occurred, where able to determine.

TRADER.name: TRADER
TRADER.comment: The account address of the trader performing the swap action.

TOKEN_IN_AMOUNT.name: TOKEN_IN_AMOUNT
TOKEN_IN_AMOUNT.comment:
The amount of the token being swapped that the trader is receiving from the pool (buying).
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

TOKEN_IN_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN_IN_CONTRACT
TOKEN_IN_CONTRACT.comment:
The smart contract address for the token that is being swapped into the traders wallet.
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

TOKEN_OUT_AMOUNT.name: TOKEN_OUT_AMOUNT
TOKEN_OUT_AMOUNT.comment:
The amount of the token being swapped that the trader is sending to the pool (selling).
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

TOKEN_OUT_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN_OUT_CONTRACT
TOKEN_OUT_CONTRACT.comment:
The smart contract address for the token that is being swapped out of the traders wallet.
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

SWAPS_AGGREGATOR_ID.name: SWAPS_AGGREGATOR_ID
SWAPS_AGGREGATOR_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: FACT_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY
metadata.comment: This table parses transactions where tokens are bridged to or from the Flow network using Blocto teleport or the Celer bridge.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

BRIDGE_ADDRESS.name: BRIDGE_ADDRESS
BRIDGE_ADDRESS.comment: The contract address for the bridge used to move tokens to or from Flow.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The contract address for a token on the Flow blockchain.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The amount of the asset involved in the transaction.

AMOUNT_FEE.name: AMOUNT_FEE
AMOUNT_FEE.comment: The fee taken by the protocol or contract as part of the transaction.

SOURCE_ADDRESS.name: SOURCE_ADDRESS
SOURCE_ADDRESS.comment: Address of a FLOW wallet related to the transaction.

DESTINATION_ADDRESS.name: DESTINATION_ADDRESS
DESTINATION_ADDRESS.comment: Address of a FLOW wallet related to the transaction.

SOURCE_CHAIN.name: SOURCE_CHAIN
SOURCE_CHAIN.comment: The name of the blockchain for this address or transaction.

DESTINATION_CHAIN.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN
DESTINATION_CHAIN.comment: The name of the blockchain for this address or transaction.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The name of the bridge or protocol used.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: FACT_PRICES_OHLC_HOURLY
metadata.comment: A comprehensive fact table holding id and provider specific open, high, low, close hourly prices, from multiple providers. This data set includes raw, non-transformed data coming directly from the provider APIs and rows are not intended to be unique. As a result, there may be data quality issues persisting in the APIs that flow through to this fact based model. If you are interested in using a curated data set instead, please utilize ez_prices_hourly.

ASSET_ID.name: ASSET_ID
ASSET_ID.comment: The unique identifier representing the asset.

HOUR.name: HOUR
HOUR.comment: Hour that the price was recorded at.

OPEN.name: OPEN
OPEN.comment: Opening price of the recorded hour in USD.

HIGH.name: HIGH
HIGH.comment: Highest price of the recorded hour in USD

LOW.name: LOW
LOW.comment: Lowest price of the recorded hour in USD

CLOSE.name: CLOSE
CLOSE.comment: Closing price of the recorded hour in USD

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

FACT_PRICES_OHLC_HOURLY_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

metadata.name: NFT_MOMENT_EDITIONS_S
metadata.comment: Cleaned EditionCreated events.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

EVENT_CONTRACT.name: EVENT_CONTRACT
EVENT_CONTRACT.comment: The contract called for this event. This is equivalent to the Contract column on Flowscan and is a concatenation of the contract's account address and primary name.

EDITION_ID.name: EDITION_ID
EDITION_ID.comment: The numeric ID for the edition of moments. This is likely a bundle of moment NFTs within the same type of play, differentiated by some trait like rarity.

MAX_MINT_SIZE.name: MAX_MINT_SIZE
MAX_MINT_SIZE.comment: The gross NFTs minted for the batch.

PLAY_ID.name: PLAY_ID
PLAY_ID.comment: The numeric ID of the play for the moment. For example, with topshot moment ABC may be a version of Play 123. The play id is 123 and moment metadata is associated with the play, rather than the moment.

SERIES_ID.name: SERIES_ID
SERIES_ID.comment: The numeric ID for the series of moments or plays. This corresponds with the series column found in some metadata tables.

SET_ID.name: SET_ID
SET_ID.comment: The numeric ID for the set. This corresponds with the set name found in some metadata columns.

TIER.name: TIER
TIER.comment: The tiered rarity of the edition.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Time the record was inserted into snowflake

metadata.name: NFT_UFC_STRIKE_METADATA
metadata.comment: UFC Strike NFT Metadata

NFT_ID.name: NFT_ID
NFT_ID.comment: The id of the NFT, usually a number.

SERIAL_NUMBER.name: SERIAL_NUMBER
SERIAL_NUMBER.comment: The serial number for the Moment, such as 25, within the collection.

OWNER.name: OWNER
OWNER.comment: The last known owner of this NFT at time of data pull

LISTING_ID.name: LISTING_ID
LISTING_ID.comment: The listing ID used by the NFT marketplace.

SET_NAME.name: SET_NAME
SET_NAME.comment: Name of the set in which the moment was dropped.

METADATA.name: METADATA
METADATA.comment: A JSON object containing moment or play metadata.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Time the record was inserted into snowflake

_FILENAME.name: _FILENAME
_FILENAME.comment: The filename of the source of the data

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

NFT_UFC_STRIKE_METADATA_ID.name: NFT_UFC_STRIKE_METADATA_ID
NFT_UFC_STRIKE_METADATA_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: FACT_BLOCKS
metadata.comment: This table contains block level data for this EVM blockchain. This table can be used to analyze trends at a block level, for example gas fees vs. total transactions over time. For more information, please see [Etherscan Resources](https://etherscan.io/directory/Learning_Resources/Ethereum) or [The Ethereum Organization](https://ethereum.org/en/developers/docs/blocks/)

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_HASH.name: BLOCK_HASH
BLOCK_HASH.comment: The hash of the block header for a given block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

NETWORK.name: NETWORK
NETWORK.comment: The network on the blockchain used by a transaction.

TX_COUNT.name: TX_COUNT
TX_COUNT.comment: Total number of transactions within a block.

SIZE.name: SIZE
SIZE.comment: Block size, which is determined by a given block's gas limit.

MINER.name: MINER
MINER.comment: The address of the beneficiary to whom the mining rewards were given

MIX_HASH.name: MIX_HASH
MIX_HASH.comment: A string of a 256-bit hash encoded as a hexadecimal

EXTRA_DATA.name: EXTRA_DATA
EXTRA_DATA.comment: Any data included by the miner for a given block.

PARENT_HASH.name: PARENT_HASH
PARENT_HASH.comment: The hash of the block from which a given block is generated. Also known as the parent block.

GAS_USED.name: GAS_USED
GAS_USED.comment: Total gas used in the block.

GAS_LIMIT.name: GAS_LIMIT
GAS_LIMIT.comment: Total gas limit provided by all transactions in the block.

BASE_FEE_PER_GAS.name: BASE_FEE_PER_GAS
BASE_FEE_PER_GAS.comment: A string of the base fee encoded in hexadecimal format. Please note that this response field will not be included in a block requested before the EIP-1559 upgrade

DIFFICULTY.name: DIFFICULTY
DIFFICULTY.comment: The effort required to mine the block.

TOTAL_DIFFICULTY.name: TOTAL_DIFFICULTY
TOTAL_DIFFICULTY.comment: Total difficulty of the chain at a given block.

SHA3_UNCLES.name: SHA3_UNCLES
SHA3_UNCLES.comment: The mechanism which Ethereum Javascript RLP encodes an empty string.

UNCLE_BLOCKS.name: UNCLE_BLOCKS
UNCLE_BLOCKS.comment: Uncle blocks occur when two blocks are mined and broadcasted at the same time, with the same block number. The block validated across the most nodes will be added to the primary chain, and the other one becomes an uncle block. Miners do receive rewards for uncle blocks.

NONCE.name: NONCE
NONCE.comment: Block nonce is a value used during mining to demonstrate proof of work for a given block.

RECEIPTS_ROOT.name: RECEIPTS_ROOT
RECEIPTS_ROOT.comment: The root of the state trie.

STATE_ROOT.name: STATE_ROOT
STATE_ROOT.comment: The root of the final state trie of the block

TRANSACTIONS_ROOT.name: TRANSACTIONS_ROOT
TRANSACTIONS_ROOT.comment: The root of the transaction trie of the block

LOGS_BLOOM.name: LOGS_BLOOM
LOGS_BLOOM.comment: The bloom filter for the logs of the block.

FACT_BLOCKS_ID.name: FACT_BLOCKS_ID
FACT_BLOCKS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: DIM_EVM_EVENT_ABIS
metadata.comment: This table contains the individual event abis for each all contracts that we have ABIs for. Proxies are rolled up to their implementation.

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: The blockchain that the event abi is for.

PARENT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: PARENT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS
PARENT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract that the event abi is for.

SIMPLE_EVENT_NAME.name: SIMPLE_EVENT_NAME
SIMPLE_EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event along with its parameters.

EVENT_SIGNATURE.name: EVENT_SIGNATURE
EVENT_SIGNATURE.comment: The keccak256 signature of the simple event name. This can be used to filter for events in the logs table on topics[0].

START_BLOCK.name: START_BLOCK
START_BLOCK.comment: The block number of the creation of the contract containing the event. This could be from a proxy or an implementation.

END_BLOCK.name: END_BLOCK
END_BLOCK.comment: The block number before a similar event was created in the contract. This could be from a proxy or an implementation.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

DIM_EVM_EVENT_ABIS_ID.name: DIM_EVM_EVENT_ABIS_ID
DIM_EVM_EVENT_ABIS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

metadata.name: FACT_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment: This table contains flattened event logs from transactions on this EVM blockchain. Transactions may have multiple events, which are denoted by the event index for a transaction hash. Therefore, this table is unique on the combination of transaction hash and event index. Please see `fact_decoded_event_logs` or `ez_decoded_event_logs` for the decoded event logs.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

TOPIC_0.name: TOPIC_0
TOPIC_0.comment: The first topic of the event, which is a unique identifier for the event.

TOPIC_1.name: TOPIC_1
TOPIC_1.comment: The second topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_2.name: TOPIC_2
TOPIC_2.comment: The third topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_3.name: TOPIC_3
TOPIC_3.comment: The fourth topic of the event, if applicable.

EVENT_REMOVED.name: EVENT_REMOVED
EVENT_REMOVED.comment: Whether the event has been removed from the transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Whether the transaction was successful, returned as a boolean.

FACT_EVENT_LOGS_ID.name: FACT_EVENT_LOGS_ID
FACT_EVENT_LOGS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

TX_STATUS.name: TX_STATUS
TX_STATUS.comment: This column is being deprecated for standardization purposes. Please update your queries to use the `tx_succeeded` column instead by March 10, 2025.

_LOG_ID.name: _LOG_ID
_LOG_ID.comment: This column is being removed for standardization purposes. Please remove or update your queries with `CONCAT(tx_hash, '_', event_index)` to reconstruct `_log_id` by March 10, 2025.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment:
'For information on how to submit a contract for decoding, as well as how ABIs are sourced, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/). This model contains decoded event logs for contracts that we have an ABI for. Please note, this table does not include all event logs, only those that we have an ABI for. The `decoded_log` column is the easiest place to query decoded data. It is a JSON object, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters. You can select from this column using the following sample format, `decoded_log:from::string` or more generally, `decoded_log:<event_param>::datatype`. See below for a full sample query. The `full_decoded_logs` column contains the same information, as well as additional fields such as the datatype of the decoded data. You may need to laterally flatten this column to query the data.
      
Sample query for USDC Transfer events:
```sql select  tx_hash, block_number, contract_address, decoded_log:from::string as from_address, decoded_log:to::string as to_address, decoded_log:value::integer as value from ethereum.core.fact_decoded_event_logs where contract_address = lower('0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48') and block_number between 16400000 and 16405000 and event_name = 'Transfer' limit 50```'

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

TOPIC_0.name: TOPIC_0
TOPIC_0.comment: The first topic of the event, which is a unique identifier for the event.

TOPIC_1.name: TOPIC_1
TOPIC_1.comment: The second topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_2.name: TOPIC_2
TOPIC_2.comment: The third topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_3.name: TOPIC_3
TOPIC_3.comment: The fourth topic of the event, if applicable.

EVENT_REMOVED.name: EVENT_REMOVED
EVENT_REMOVED.comment: Whether the event has been removed from the transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the contract call.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Status of the transaction.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event, as defined in the contract ABI.

FULL_DECODED_LOG.name: FULL_DECODED_LOG
FULL_DECODED_LOG.comment: The full decoded log, including the event name, the event parameters, and the data type of the event parameters.

DECODED_LOG.name: DECODED_LOG
DECODED_LOG.comment: The flattened decoded log, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters.

CONTRACT_NAME.name: CONTRACT_NAME
CONTRACT_NAME.comment: The name of the contract, if the contract has a name() function.

EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS_ID.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS_ID
EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

TX_STATUS.name: TX_STATUS
TX_STATUS.comment: This column is being deprecated for standardization purposes. Please update your queries by March 10, 2025.
